LAW AND MOTION 302,DEFENDANT DENISE ARMSTRONG'S MOTION FOR DETERMINATION OF GOOD FAITH SETTLEMENT PURSUANT TO CODE OF CIVIL PROCEDURE 877.6 (A)(2). DEFENDANT DENISE MARIE ARMSTRONG'S UNOPPOSED MOTION FOR DETERMINATION OF GOOD FAITH SETTLEMENT PURSUANT TO CODE OF CIVIL PROCEDURE 877.6 (A)(2) IS GRANTED. GOOD CAUSE APPEARING, THE COURT FINDS THE SETTLEMENT BETWEEN PLAINTIFFS ISAIAH VELARDE AND ANDREA ACOSTA AND DEFENDANT DENISE MARIE ARMSTRONG, ENTERED JULY 22, 2024, IS IN GOOD FAITH WITHIN THE MEANING OF CODE OF CIVIL PROCEDURE SECTIONS 877 AND 876.6. MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT DENISE ARMSTRONG's Motion For Determination Of Good Faith Settlement Pursuant To Code Of Civil Procedure 877.6 (A)(2).

Defendant Denise Marie Armstrong's unopposed Motion For Determination Of Good Faith Settlement Pursuant To Code Of Civil Procedure 877.6 (A)(2) is GRANTED.

Good cause appearing, the court finds the settlement between Plaintiffs Isaiah Velarde and Andrea Acosta and Defendant Denise Marie Armstrong, entered July 22, 2024, is in good faith within the meaning of Code of Civil procedure sections 877 and 876.6.

Order is signed.

MOTION TO STRIKE Amended COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Oct-10-2024 Denied
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances (via Zoom videoconference):
Atty. SCOTT RIGHTHAND, Esq. of Law Offices of Scott Righthand, APC (tel: 415-544-0115) for Plaintiff.
Atty. JAMES IREY, Esq. of Yukevich Cavanaugh (tel: 213-362-7777) for Defendant Volvo Car USA, LLC.
Atty. FRANCISCO CHIQUILLO, Esq. of Homan, Stone & Rossi (tel: 909-307-9380) for Defendant Denise Marie Armstrong.

Hearing Re: DEFENDANT VOLVO CAR USA LLC's MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST Amended COMPLAINT.

Matter argued and submitted. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Volvo Car USA, LLC's "motion to strike punitive damages allegations" from the first amended complaint (FAC) is denied. The FAC adequately alleges bases for punitive damages at the pleading stage (see, e.g., 7:14-8:11); discovery can be taken.

Counsel for the prevailing party is to prepare and submit a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling, with a cover letter stating compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr.; Clerk: V. Da Fonseca; Not reported. (302/RBU)

DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Oct-10-2024 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances (via Zoom videoconference):
Atty. SCOTT RIGHTHAND, Esq. of Law Offices of Scott Righthand, APC (tel: 415-544-0115) for Plaintiff.
Atty. JAMES IREY, Esq. of Yukevich Cavanaugh (tel: 213-362-7777) for Defendant Volvo Car USA, LLC.
Atty. FRANCISCO CHIQUILLO, Esq. of Homan, Stone & Rossi (tel: 909-307-9380) for Defendant Denise Marie Armstrong.

Hearing Re: DEFENDANT VOLVO CAR USA LLC's DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT.

Matter argued and submitted. Counsel Scott Righthand requests 30 days to amend the complaint. The Court grants the request. The Court adopts its tentative ruling as modified:

Volvo Car USA, LLC's demurrer to the first amended complaint (FAC) is overruled with one exception noted below. The FAC pleads that Isaiah Velarde stopped his Volvo XC90 to turn and another vehicle traveling the same direction impacted it. (Id. at 3:20-4:6.) The Volvo's steering wheel turned involuntarily, snapping Velarde's right arm to expose bone. (Id. at 3:20-4:6, 11:15-18.) Volvo first demurs that to allege products liability the FAC had to specify what the defective parts were. (Dmr. 2:13-16.) But Volvo cites no authority for this notion and it is contrary to California's liberal pleading standards. Volvo also demurs that to allege breach of warranty the FAC had to specify when Velarde's Volvo was purchased and whether it was new. (Dmr. 3:1-2.) Again, Volvo cites no authority for this notion and it is contrary to California's liberal pleading standards. Plaintiffs are granted leave to amend within 30 days to plead warranty terms, as to which demurrer is sustained. (Dmr. 3:4-5.) Punitive damages are addressed on the accompanying motion to strike.

Order signed.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr.; Clerk: V. Da Fonseca; Not reported. (302/RBU)
